name: Quindio (Colombia)

colors.symbolism: the coffee plantations and forested mountains of the Coffee Axis
the gold and other mineral wealth of the territory
the dignity, sovereignty and seriousness of the Quindio people

description: Three equal vertical stripes, green at the hoist, yellow in the centre, and purple at the fly.
history: Quindio was separated from Caldas to become a department in 1966; its tricolour flag in green, yellow and purple was adopted to symbolise the coffee region's nature, mineral wealth and dignity.

features.symbolism: green for the coffee region's mountains and forests, yellow for the gold mined in the area, and purple for the dignity and authority of the people
